title: Slack Integration Guide
content:
## Overview

Slack is a business communication platform that provides real-time messaging, channels, file sharing, and integrations with thousands of tools. Slack is widely used by security operations teams for real-time collaboration, alert notifications, and incident coordination. The T1 Agentics integration with Slack enables SOC teams to send security alerts and notifications to Slack channels, create dedicated incident channels, and facilitate real-time communication during security incident response.

**Vendor:** Salesforce (Slack)
**Category:** Communication & Collaboration
**Authentication:** Bot Token (OAuth 2.0)

## Prerequisites

Before configuring the Slack integration in T1 Agentics, ensure you have:

- A Slack workspace with administrative access
- A Slack App created at [https://api.slack.com/apps](https://api.slack.com/apps):
  - Create a new app **From scratch** or **From a manifest**
  - Navigate to **OAuth & Permissions** and add the following Bot Token Scopes:
    - `chat:write` (send messages)
    - `channels:read` (list public channels)
    - `channels:manage` (create channels)
    - `channels:join` (join public channels)
    - `groups:read` (list private channels, if needed)
    - `users:read` (look up users)
    - `files:write` (upload files/attachments)
  - Install the app to your workspace and copy the **Bot User OAuth Token** (starts with `xoxb-`)
- The Slack app must be invited to any private channels it needs to post to
- Network access from T1 Agentics to the Slack API (`https://slack.com/api`)

## Configuration in T1 Agentics

1. Navigate to **Connect > Marketplace** in the T1 Agentics sidebar
2. Search for **Slack** or browse the **Communication** category
3. Click on the Slack card and select **Configure**
4. Enter your connection details:
   - **Connection Name**: A descriptive label (e.g., "Slack - SOC Workspace")
   - **Bot Token**: Your Slack Bot User OAuth Token (`xoxb-...`)
   - **Default Channel**: (Optional) The default channel for notifications (e.g., `#security-alerts`)
5. Click **Test Connection** to verify the bot token is valid
6. Click **Save** to activate the integration
7. The integration will appear in your **Connect > Active Integrations** list

## Available Actions

| Action | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| Send Message | Generic | Post a message to a Slack channel |
| Send Direct Message | Generic | Send a direct message to a specific user |
| Create Channel | Generic | Create a new Slack channel (for incident response) |
| Archive Channel | Generic | Archive a channel after incident resolution |
| Set Channel Topic | Generic | Update a channel's topic (e.g., incident status) |
| Set Channel Purpose | Generic | Update a channel's purpose description |
| Invite User to Channel | Generic | Add a user to a specific channel |
| List Channels | Investigate | List available channels in the workspace |
| List Users | Investigate | List users in the workspace |
| Get User Info | Investigate | Retrieve profile information for a specific user |
| Upload File | Generic | Upload a file or report to a channel |
| Add Reaction | Generic | Add an emoji reaction to a message |

## Common Use Cases

- **Real-time security alerting**: Send formatted security alerts to dedicated Slack channels (e.g., `#security-alerts`, `#soc-critical`) when T1 Agentics detects high-severity incidents
- **Incident channel creation**: Automatically create a dedicated Slack channel for each major security incident (e.g., `#inc-2024-001-ransomware`) and invite relevant responders
- **Investigation updates**: Post investigation progress, containment actions, and IOC findings to incident channels as T1 Agentics playbooks execute
- **Analyst notification**: Send direct messages to on-call analysts or team leads when critical alerts require immediate attention
- **Post-incident communication**: Share incident reports and post-mortem summaries with stakeholders through Slack channels

## Troubleshooting

| Issue | Solution |
|-------|----------|
| `not_authed` error | The bot token is invalid or has been revoked. Regenerate the token by reinstalling the Slack app |
| `channel_not_found` | The bot must be a member of private channels. Invite the bot to the channel, or use the channel ID instead of the channel name |
| `missing_scope` | The Slack app lacks a required OAuth scope. Add the missing scope in the app's OAuth & Permissions settings and reinstall the app |
| Messages not appearing | Verify the bot has been invited to the target channel. Public channels may still require the bot to join first |
| Rate limiting (429) | Slack enforces per-method rate limits (typically 1 request per second for posting). Implement retry logic with the `Retry-After` header |
| File upload fails | Verify the `files:write` scope is granted and the file size is within Slack's limits (varies by plan) |
